name: workplace_safety_water_allocation_model_cedar_rig_utl
premise: A Workplace Safety response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving workplace safety water allocation model cedar rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next shift change. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Hector Mensah
agents.identity: I am Hector Mensah, accountable for a defensible call in Workplace Safety when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Inez Serrano
agents.identity: I am Inez Serrano, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Workplace Safety.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next shift change. Evidence is partial. Hector Mensah must decide under rights vs utility. What does Hector Mensah do?
